author: @edgarmoctezuma7777
text: For my DIY magnetic stir plate i used a Notebook Cooler Pad aluminum top with (3) mini fans. These fans are rated at 1500RPM which produce a very moderate speed in case you don't have or want to buy a potentiometer. I just cut out the middle aluminum grill, epoxy a metal washer or (knock-out plate, metal hole cover round disc) to the center & a small rectangular neodymium magnet, computer hardrive magnets are also popular. Careful with the fan fins, they are delicate. Works a base as well. You might need a spacer between the magnet and the jar, i made a donut shaped spacer out of some 3/8'' thick cork panel i had laying around. Ebay # 230827992876 for the notebook cooler pad. Hope this helps someone out !!! PEACE O(*_*)O

author: @manwar999
text: ah yes the webs...i settled on graphite powder mixed in with contact cement for a quick flexible fast drying conductive circuit painted on paper and got an ohm reading of approx 500 ohms per 1 1/2 inch...and now since your post on ''Gary'' im now experimenting with the neutral line...will post some findings soon...very exciting
